framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.what_it_is: Use high-throughput DFT calculations to compute and publish the properties of thousands of materials in a free, queryable database or open-source library.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn deep quantum-mechanical materials theory into infrastructure other scientists and engineers rely on daily, rather than keeping the expertise locked in one lab's private results.

paths.what_it_is: Write and maintain open-source molecular dynamics or simulation-analysis software that becomes a standard tool across the materials modeling community.
paths.why_it_signals: Sustaining production-grade scientific software for a global user base proves engineering discipline layered on top of materials science depth - a rarer combination than research output alone.

paths.path: Master atom-probe tomography and teach it openly
paths.what_it_is: Build hands-on command of atom-probe tomography - specimen preparation, laser-assisted acquisition, 3D reconstruction - then publish analysis methods and train other researchers in the technique.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn a demanding experimental method into a capability others can learn from you, not just something you quietly do well on your own samples.
